V-M-037.checks: Admin frontend must become compact and usable from a phone for operational status, user search, devices, payments/analytics, and system health.
V-M-037.test-files: frontend-admin/src/components/Layout.tsx frontend-admin/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx frontend-admin/src/pages/Users.tsx frontend-admin/src/pages/Devices.tsx frontend-admin/src/pages/MTProto.tsx frontend-admin/src/pages/Analytics.tsx frontend-admin/src/pages/Servers.tsx frontend-admin/src/pages/Plans.tsx frontend-admin/src/components/StatCard.tsx frontend-admin/src/index.css frontend-admin/src/lib/api.ts frontend-admin/src/types/index.ts scripts/phase54-admin-matrix-ui-smoke.mjs scripts/phase74-admin-shell-navigation-smoke.mjs scripts/phase75-admin-users-devices-smoke.mjs
V-M-037.commands: pass-local 2026-06-05: Phase-75 frontend-admin build and admin Users/Devices smoke passed for single Users nav, bounded expanded device lists, reachable actions, readable confirmations, and protected backend/deploy/runtime/user frontend guard.

levels.module-local: TypeScript/Vite build and static source review after every Phase-24 frontend-admin change.
levels.wave-level: Mobile smoke across overview, users, devices, and analytics/payment routes after each completed UI wave.
levels.phase-level: Final Phase-24 gate requires build, mobile smoke, confirmation-path evidence, and deploy/install no-diff evidence before V-M-037 can move to pass-local.

module-checks.check-2: Mobile overview surfaces service health, users, subscriptions, revenue, VPN/system status, and urgent counts without desktop-only layout.
module-checks.check-3: User search and device registry are readable on phone viewports without table-only primary workflows.
module-checks.check-4: Device block, unblock, rotate, and revoke are reachable and require confirmation.
module-checks.check-5: Audit-sensitive admin actions preserve current backend audit expectations and show refreshed success/error feedback.
module-checks.check-6: No deploy/install scripts are modified during Phase-24 execution.
module-checks.check-7: Admin API base path and device endpoint names remain compatible with M-006 and M-024 unless separately approved.
module-checks.check-8: Mobile smoke auth fixture uses a test admin state and does not require storing real production tokens in evidence.
module-checks.check-9: Phase-54 Matrix mobile admin shell keeps navigation, search, pagination, filters, and action controls compact at 360px without long unstructured lists.
module-checks.check-10: Phase-54 tables, graphs, and investigation panels use bounded internal scroll or pagination instead of document-level horizontal scroll.
module-checks.check-11: Phase-54 confirms confirmation dialogs/sheets remain readable, focused, and visually above Matrix overlays.
module-checks.check-12: Phase-74 admin navigation removes Tariffs from visible mobile/tablet/desktop nav while keeping Dashboard, Users, MTProto, Servers/Nodes, Analytics, and Logout reachable.
module-checks.check-13: Phase-74 logout position is directly below Nodes in the admin rail so it stays visible without requiring a long scroll.
module-checks.check-14: Phase-74 scroll repair proves mouse-wheel and touch scrolling operate over admin content panes, with the rail bounded instead of owning the only useful scroll path.
module-checks.check-15: Phase-75 phone/tablet admin navigation exposes one Users section and does not show a separate Devices tab after the merge.
module-checks.check-16: Phase-75 expanded user device lists must stay bounded and scrollable so many devices do not create an unmanageable long page.
module-checks.check-17: Phase-75 device actions remain reachable from mobile within the expanded user row or compact detail surface.
module-checks.check-18: Phase-75 confirmation surfaces remain readable above Matrix overlays and name the exact device target.
module-checks.check-19: Phase-75 preserves search for users and a practical path to inspect devices for the selected user on phone viewports.

deterministic-assertions.assert-1: cd frontend-admin && npm run build exits 0.
deterministic-assertions.assert-2: Every checked viewport records document.documentElement.scrollWidth <= window.innerWidth.
deterministic-assertions.assert-3: Device action controls do not call blockMutation.mutate, unblockMutation.mutate, rotateMutation.mutate, or revokeMutation.mutate directly from row buttons.
deterministic-assertions.assert-4: Routes /, /users, /devices compatibility redirect, and /analytics remain registered in frontend-admin/src/main.tsx; visible compact navigation exposes /users without a separate /devices tab after Phase-75.
deterministic-assertions.assert-5: deploy, install.sh, nginx, docker-compose.yml, frontend-admin/Dockerfile, and frontend-admin/nginx.conf have no Phase-24 diff.
deterministic-assertions.assert-6: Phase-54 checked routes /, /users, /devices, /mtproto, /servers, /plans, and /analytics report document.documentElement.scrollWidth <= window.innerWidth on mobile viewports.
deterministic-assertions.assert-7: Phase-54 checked routes expose their primary search, pagination, or action controls within compact mobile surfaces.
deterministic-assertions.assert-8: Phase-74 smoke confirms no visible "Тарифы" navigation item or stale Tariffs card remains in admin shell output.
deterministic-assertions.assert-9: Phase-74 smoke confirms admin content containers expose normal vertical overflow/scroll markers and do not depend on rail-only wheel scrolling.
deterministic-assertions.assert-10: Phase-74 smoke confirms admin-rail has explicit height/overflow bounds compatible with phone, tablet, and desktop layouts.
deterministic-assertions.assert-11: Phase-75 smoke confirms no visible "Устройства" navigation item remains after the merge.
deterministic-assertions.assert-12: Phase-75 smoke confirms the Users page carries unified-inventory and expandable-device-row markers.
deterministic-assertions.assert-13: Phase-75 smoke confirms expanded device lists have bounded-scroll or pagination markers for many-device users.
deterministic-assertions.assert-14: Phase-75 smoke confirms one-device confirmation guards remain in the unified Users route.
deterministic-assertions.assert-15: Phase-75 smoke confirms direct /devices handling is a safe redirect/compat path, not a duplicate primary nav surface.

phase-74-admin-shell-responsive-gates.gate-1: Desktop admin shell keeps Logout under Nodes, not below long secondary content.
phase-74-admin-shell-responsive-gates.gate-2: Visible admin navigation does not include Tariffs/Plans after Phase-74, while operational routes remain reachable and compact.
phase-74-admin-shell-responsive-gates.gate-3: Content-area wheel scrolling and rail clipping are asserted statically by Phase-74 smoke markers.
phase-74-admin-shell-responsive-gates.gate-4: Phase-74 changes are frontend-admin only; no backend API, billing provider, deploy, install, nginx, docker, or runtime behavior changed.

phase-75-unified-inventory-responsive-ga.gate-1: Phone and tablet admin navigation must keep Users, MTProto, Nodes, Analytics, and Logout reachable while removing the separate Devices nav item.
phase-75-unified-inventory-responsive-ga.gate-2: Expanded device lists inside Users must be compact, bounded, and usable at 360x740, 390x844, 430x932, 768x1024, and 1440x900.
phase-75-unified-inventory-responsive-ga.gate-3: Device action buttons and confirmation sheet/dialog must remain visible and touch-safe without document-level horizontal scroll.
phase-75-unified-inventory-responsive-ga.gate-4: Phase-75 must not weaken Phase-74 content scroll or rail bounds.

static-probes.probe-1: Review frontend-admin/src/pages/Devices.tsx for confirmation state, dialog/sheet/modal, or equivalent explicit confirmation before mutation.
static-probes.probe-2: Review frontend-admin/src/components/Layout.tsx for mobile navigation that does not preserve the 320px sidebar as the primary phone path.
static-probes.probe-3: Review frontend-admin/src/pages/Users.tsx and frontend-admin/src/pages/Devices.tsx for compact rows/cards that are usable without table-only horizontal scrolling.
static-probes.probe-4: Review frontend-admin/src/pages/Analytics.tsx for phone-first KPI summaries before chart-heavy detail.

viewport-matrix.viewport-1: Overview loads without horizontal document scroll and exposes system health plus urgent user/device navigation.
viewport-matrix.viewport-2: Search, counters, role/status, identity, and pagination fit without a desktop-only table dependency.
viewport-matrix.viewport-3: Device rows, signals, and block/unblock/rotate/revoke confirmation paths fit within the viewport.
viewport-matrix.viewport-4: Payment and analytics status remains readable without charts dominating the first phone viewport.

phase-54-viewport-matrix.viewport-1: Matrix dashboard health/KPI rows remain readable and primary operational links are visible.
phase-54-viewport-matrix.viewport-2: Search, filters, user rows, role/status, and pagination fit compactly without document horizontal scroll.
phase-54-viewport-matrix.viewport-3: Device rows and block/unblock/rotate/revoke confirmation paths remain reachable and non-overlapping.
phase-54-viewport-matrix.viewport-4: Assignment inventory, embedded analytics, alerts, IP history, promotion tag, and reissue/revoke controls stay usable.
phase-54-viewport-matrix.viewport-5: Nodes, routes, forms, and delete/edit actions stay bounded and confirmation-safe.
phase-54-viewport-matrix.viewport-6: Canonical tariff matrix remains readable without accidental edit/delete controls.
phase-54-viewport-matrix.viewport-7: Business charts and summaries stay compact and do not dominate the first phone viewport.

smoke-fixture.auth-fixture: Use a non-production persisted admin-auth/localStorage fixture or a test backend session; never record real admin_token or refresh token values.
smoke-fixture.api-fixture: Use local backend, mocked network responses, or existing dev fixtures sufficient to render stats, users, devices, billing, and health states.
smoke-fixture.state-fixture: Exercise loading, empty, populated, success feedback, error feedback, and confirmation states where practical.

scenarios.scenario-1: Admin can inspect system state and manage a device from a phone without switching to desktop layout.
scenarios.scenario-2: Admin can search a user and read role/status/account context from compact rows at 360px width.
scenarios.scenario-3: Admin can open a device action confirmation that names the target peer before mutation.
scenarios.scenario-4: Critical device actions are hidden in a wide table or unavailable on mobile.
scenarios.scenario-5: An admin action lacks confirmation or loses audit visibility.
scenarios.scenario-6: A compact UI change modifies deploy/install scripts, backend business rules, or billing provider behavior.

telemetry.telemetry-1: Record route, viewport width/height, scrollWidth, innerWidth, and primary visible controls for each smoke step.
telemetry.telemetry-2: Record the device action name, target display label, confirmation surface presence, and post-action feedback behavior without storing peer keys or tokens.
telemetry.telemetry-3: Record build command, exit code, and git diff no-deploy result.

redaction.redact-1: Do not record admin_token, admin_refresh_token, Authorization headers, cookies, private keys, full VPN configs, raw .env values, payment secrets, or database URLs.
redaction.redact-2: Use anonymized emails and device names in screenshots or textual evidence when possible.

V-M-037.autonomous-execution-safety: Autonomous execution is allowed only for frontend-admin UI files and matching MyGRACE docs. Backend business rules, billing provider behavior, Phase-25 auth/session changes, deploy scripts, install scripts, docker, and nginx files are excluded.

planned-evidence.evidence-1: 2026-05-10: npm run build passed in frontend-admin.
planned-evidence.evidence-2: 2026-05-10: Chrome/CDP mobile smoke passed for /, /users, /devices, and /analytics at 360x740, 390x844, and 430x932; every route reported scrollWidth equal to innerWidth and mobile nav present.
planned-evidence.evidence-3: 2026-05-10: Device action smoke clicked Block, opened confirmation text containing the target device/user/scope, and observed 0 mutation requests before confirm.
planned-evidence.evidence-4: 2026-05-10: Static review found no direct Mutation.mutate calls from device row buttons.
planned-evidence.evidence-6: 2026-06-03: Phase-54 verification design added mobile Matrix route, viewport, pagination, bounded scroll, confirmation, and protected-surface gates.
planned-evidence.evidence-7: 2026-06-03: Phase-54 local execution passed frontend-admin build and smoke markers [MobileAdminConsole][Phase54][ROUTE_VIEWPORT_SAFE], [PRIMARY_ACTIONS_REACHABLE], [TABLES_SCROLL_CONTAINED], and [CONFIRMATIONS_READABLE].
planned-evidence.evidence-8: 2026-06-05: Phase-74 smoke passed [MobileAdminConsole][phase74][ADMIN_LOGOUT_UNDER_NODES], [CONTENT_SCROLL_WHEEL_SAFE], and [RAIL_BOUNDS_SAFE] with no visible Tariffs nav.
planned-evidence.evidence-9: 2026-06-05: Phase-75 smoke passed [MobileAdminConsole][phase75][SINGLE_USERS_NAV], [EXPANDED_DEVICE_LIST_BOUNDED], [DEVICE_ACTIONS_REACHABLE], and [CONFIRMATIONS_READABLE] with /devices redirected to /users.

phase-76-verification-design.scope: Mobile admin console regression verification across Phase-74 and Phase-75.
phase-76-verification-design.gate-1: Phone, tablet, and desktop admin shell must keep Dashboard, Users, MTProto, Nodes, Analytics, and Logout reachable without visible Tariffs or separate Devices navigation.
phase-76-verification-design.gate-2: Expanded user device rows must remain bounded, scrollable, action-reachable, and confirmation-safe on mobile.
phase-76-verification-design.gate-3: Phase-54, Phase-58, Phase-59, Phase-61, and Phase-62 admin regressions must remain green in the Phase-76 bundle.

V-M-037.phase-76-evidence: Consolidated Phase-76 gate passed for mobile admin console: Phase-74/75 smokes plus Phase-54/58/59/62 regressions kept the unified Users inventory, bounded device rows, logout placement, content scroll, and confirmation surfaces intact.
